It's embarrassing to say the least. Once upon a time we had the biggest navy in the world. It helped to liberate the Falkland Islands in 1982. Now? Embarrassing.

In yet another sign of the deep freeze consuming the transatlantic 'special relationship' the president lashed out at the Royal Navy's capabilities in a rant against US Nato allies. He claimed the UK had offered to send its two carriers, HMS Queen Elizabeth and the fleet flagship HMS Prince of Wales, once the fighting in the Middle East was 'over', and had told Keir Starmer 'don't bother.' The president's remarks come after Downing Street previously rejected Trump's claims that it offered to send any aircraft carriers to the war at all. Sir Keir is becoming increasingly reluctant to allow the UK to be dragged into the conflict which has triggered a surge in energy prices.

Heir hunters tracing the family of a British woman who died without a will uncovered secrets that had remained hidden for decades - while giving her long-lost cousin the missing details she had spent years desperately searching for. When Lynn Howell, from Great Yarmouth, Norfolk, died in 2022 aged 75, she left a modest estate with no known living relatives, prompting probate research firm Fraser and Fraser to look into the case. But the team - best known for featuring on the BBC One show Heir Hunters - swiftly hit an obstacle after struggling to locate the birth certificate of Lynn's mother, Joan Phyllis Beard.
  • 1 comment
  • share How search for dead Brit's family uncovered relatives 5,000 miles away

An independent inquiry into one of the most violent days of the 1980s Miners' strike has been launched, with the chair vowing to 'establish the truth'. The Battle of Orgreave saw bloody clashes between police and pickets outside the Orgreave Coking Plant in South Yorkshire on June 18, 1984, leaving scores of mineworkers injured. The Bishop chairing the inquiry said 'serious questions' about the events remain, while the Government said miners and campaigners will finally get the answers they deserve as it launched a formal inquiry.

  • What a weirdo. There's times to estrange yourself from very abusive parents. This isn't one of them. So she had strict parents. Big deal. She got out on her own so she didn't have to answer to their house, their rules. Her sister Aimee on Instagram has wish a Happy Fathers Day before to "My terrific dad." I think Heather is just an oddball to cut off all contact since age 25. My parents made questionable calls at times and even said things to me in the heat of moment or in disappointment with my actions or just old-fashioned thinking. Never, ever did I once think "How dare, they! I'm cutting off contact." I miss them every day of my life since they passed away. I would give anything to walk in the family home and have my mom ask me why I cut my hair that way and dad say I need to leave the city I'm in and come home at age 40. LOL. I would laugh and hold them as tight as I could and tell them how much I love them. I miss them so much.

Experts have revealed why an entire pack of wolves was euthanised at an animal park in Canterbury - and not just one or two unruly animals. Wildwood Trust was forced to make the 'absolute last resort' decision due to 'severe aggression' between the pack. Three out of the five European grey wolves had reportedly suffered serious injuries - as bosses at the park said the decision to put them down was made after taking extensive advice from keepers and veterinary specialists. Furious animal lovers have vented their frustrations online, demanding to know why the wolves could not have been separated or relocated.   • There are so many men that do this to women, who believe they are working towards a future together. I think women should be able to sue for fraud when this happens let's say, after five years of marriage. Why do I think this? Because the unknowing partner makes all kinds of financial decisions based on being with that partner for the foreseeable future. So when the person comes OUT, that should NOT be to the detriment of the original partner! I understand that some people have trouble being honest with themselves, or they feel they would be "shunned" if they came OUT, but we ALL have serious life issues to deal with.....we ALL face serious consequences for our decisions, and I view THIS issue the same as any other!

  • Well that's another program to miss. I'm not against lesbians, but like so much that's wrong in this woke world, I don't want it thrust in front of me in my home.

Eh, theft or assassination attempt?

A motorcyclist had his bike stolen by two thieves while he was riding it at 100kmph in Argentina. The incident took place on the Acceso Sudeste highway in Avellaneda, Buenos Aires. Footage of the ambush that was captured on the victim's helmet camera showed the rider travelling at high speeds in the fast lane when two assailants on another motorcycle suddenly drew level with him.
  • 22 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Motorcyclist has his bike stolen - while riding it at 100kmph!
